test_cases.input: Auto-generated English subtitles from a medical documentary. Multiple proper noun errors (drug names, medical terminology). Broadcast delivery target. SRT format.
test_cases.notes: Proper noun corrections were accurate for standard medical terminology. Line break rules were applied consistently at clause boundaries. Changes Log was complete and specific.

test_cases.input: Streaming platform delivery (Netflix-style) subtitles from a documentary with fast-talking interviewees. Several blocks are over-speed and need splitting. Plain text without timecodes.
test_cases.notes: Over-speed blocks were correctly identified and split with logical content breaks. Platform standard was correctly applied. Changes Log noted every split clearly.

test_cases.input: Cinema (DCP) delivery — subtitles from a documentary with Spanish dialogue originally subbed for broadcast. Need reformatting to cinema standard with narrower line length.
test_cases.notes: Cinema standard was correctly applied with narrower character limit. Some blocks required additional splits due to the reduced line length. Noted correctly that cinema standard timecode adjustments need verification in a subtitle application.
